LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST IT ON SEPTEMBER 25, 2017 IS GRANTED. BASED ON A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE STANDARD, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE CITY WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT AND THUS THE CITY'S MOTION IS NOT LIMITED TO THE TIME FRAME SET FORTH IN CCP 473(B). GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS AND PLAINTIFF TYLON HUNT HAS NOT IDENTIFIED ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE HE WOULD SUFFER BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/HK

HUNT DID NOT SERVE THE CITY WITH THE OPERATIVE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, WHICH SUPERSEDED ALL PREVIOUS PLEADINGS. (HAYES V. RISK (1967) 255 CAL.APP.2D 613, 623.) County Of Alameda'S Motion For Summary Judgment To Fourth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. RENOE E. ROSENBLIT, Esq. of San Francisco Office of City the Attorney (Tel: 415-554-3853) for Defendant City and County of San Francisco;
Atty. SAMONA L. TAYLOR, Esq. of California Office of the Attorney General (Tel: 415-510-3593) for Defendant State of California;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: County Of Alameda's Motion For Summary Judgment to Fourth Amended Complaint.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendant County of Alameda's motion for summary judgment is granted. First, plaintiff failed to file a timely opposition to the motion by June 30, 2020, as required by the parties' stipulation and Code Civ. Proc. Sec 437c(b)(2). Plaintiff filed his papers one full week late, not (as he claims) one day late. Second, plaintiff's late-filed opposition papers fail to establish that there is any material triable issue of fact supporting his claim that the County's breach of a mandatory duty caused him to lose a 2014 City of Oakland home improvement loan opportunity. Plaintiff admits that in May 1999, the County and the City and County of San Francisco recorded an acknowledgement of satisfaction of judgment and release of judgment lien, indicating full satisfaction of the child support judgment. (UMF 4; Hunt Decl. 5 ["I cannot disprove that this occurred."].) Further, the County's moving papers establish that the City of Oakland closed the loan program due to lack of funding before plaintiff paid off various other outstanding judgments and liens, which plaintiff also admits. (Hunt Decl. 6, 11.) Plaintiff's speculation that but for defendants' alleged breaches, he might have qualified for the loan before the funding for the program ran out does not amount to a viable claim. The complaint is also barred by plaintiff's failure to present a timely claim in compliance with the requirements of the Government Claims Act.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

State Of California'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jul-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. RENOE E. ROSENBLIT, Esq. of San Francisco Office of City the Attorney (Tel: 415-554-3853) for Defendant City and County of San Francisco;
Atty. SAMONA L. TAYLOR, Esq. of California Office of the Attorney General (Tel: 415-510-3593) for Defendant State of California;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: State Of California's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: The State of California's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to respond to the State's showing that it is not a proper party; that the Fourth Amended Complaint does not state a claim sufficient to satisfy the heightened pleading requirements applicable to lawsuits against public entities; and that he did not file a timely claim in compliance with the Government Claims Act. Plaintiff's one-page opposition explaining his "theory" that because the State was the ultimate recipient of the child support judgment, it "should be responsible for its consequences or reimbursement," fails to articulate any viable claim against the State or to cite any supporting authority.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Apr-24-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default, as to the defaults entered on 9/25/17 is GRANTED. The motion is timely because a void default can be vacated at any time. (See CCP 473(d); Westport Oil Co. v. Garrison (1971) 19 Cal.App.3d 974, 978.) The record fails to disclose proper service on either movant. The proof of service dated 11/6/15 indicates that plaintiff attempted to serve the summons and amended complaint on the State, but failed to comply with CCP 415.30 because plaintiff failed to provide two copies of the Notice and Acknowledgement of Receipt and a postage-paid return envelope. (Movants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. B.) That proof of service does not indicate proper service on the CUIAB either. (See also Vandeleur Dec.). The other proofs of service only indicate service of the statement of damages on the moving defendants. (Movants' Request for Judicial Notice, Exs. C and D.) In sum, there is no competent evidence that the summons and complaint or amended complaint were properly served on the movants and therefore, the motion is granted. Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Mar-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tylon Hunt, T:415.572.6144, plaintiff/opposing party appearing on his own behalf;
Jill P. Sazama, Esq., Deputy County Counsel (Alameda), T:510.272.6714, for defendant and moving party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT COUNTY OF ALAMEDA's Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default as follows:

Defendant County of Alameda's motion to set aside the September 25, 2017 entry of the default of Oakland/Alameda County Child Support Div. is granted. There has been insufficient evidence that the defaulted party is a legal entity or was properly served with a summons and complaint more than thirty days before the entry of default. Moreover, the court finds that the motion should be granted per CCP 473.5 because, even if there was a proper service of summons and complaint, the County of Alameda did not receive actual notice from such service in time to defend this lawsuit and the motion was filed with reasonable diligence less than two years after entry of the default. In all events, granting the motion comports with California's strong public policy in favor of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff Tylon Hunt has not identified any legal prejudice that he would suffer if the motion was granted.

In addition, the Court ordered the defendant to file its demurrer by March 22, 2018.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
